scenario: Discourse work session - audit the Upload contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer names Discourse as a Rails + Sidekiq app, maps every relevant directory (app/models, app/models/concerns, lib/file_store, app/jobs/{regular,scheduled,onceoff}, lib/backup_restore) with concrete paths, locates Upload at app/models/upload.rb:5, and surfaces the four shared foundations (HasUrl concern, HasPostUploadReferences concern, FileStore abstraction, UploadReference join record) — an agent can pick any entry point and proceed with only targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is cited with file:line (app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3, lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7, lib/file_store/s3_store.rb:10, app/models/upload_reference.rb:3) rather than vague layer names; nothing is left at 'the model layer'.

justification.rationale: The answer states Upload is 'the central record for every stored file' referenced by '~18 models through UploadReference, plus directly via FK columns', which implies the shared-media-record role, but it does not explain WHY storage is abstracted (local vs. S3 parity) or WHY URL is the identity key — the roles are named but the architectural motivations are mostly left implicit.

uncertainty.rationale: The orientation section presents all pieces with full confidence and flags no gaps or unverified connections the agent should check before acting; a brief note on multisite RailsMultisite is the only caveat, and it is not framed as an uncertainty.

relationship.rationale: The answer states the connecting mechanisms — polymorphic join via UploadReference, direct FK columns (image_upload_id, uploaded_avatar_id, flair_upload_id), URL/sha1 resolution through the HasUrl concern, and storage selected via Discourse.store — so it does not merely list directories; it names the routes by which dependents reach Upload, though the specific named associations per dependent area (posts, avatars, themes) are deferred to later steps.

steps.step: Map the Upload contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps every layer the rubric asks about with file:line: HasUrl concern at upload.rb:7->has_url.rb:3, all 9 associations with line numbers, lifecycle callbacks, the full contract-surface method list, and every derived/join record (OptimizedImage, OptimizedVideo, UploadReference, TopicThumbnail, ExternalUploadStub). The teammate_notes section on URL-path coupling and destroy semantics means an agent can immediately identify what a change must preserve without any exploratory re-reads.

specificity.rationale: Every contract piece is cited to an exact file:line — 'belongs_to :access_control_post (class Post, unscoped) — upload.rb:18-24', 'HasUrl — app/models/upload.rb:7 -> app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3', 'OptimizedImage app/models/optimized_image.rb:3 (create_for at :35, destroy->remove_optimized_image at :153)'; nothing is left at the 'the concerns and associations' level of vagueness.

justification.rationale: The teammate_notes explain WHY the URL is the source of truth for path computation and WHY destroy must not be bypassed, but the answer never explains WHY URL resolution is factored into a shared concern (reused by OptimizedImage) or WHY variants are separate AR records rather than inline fields — the structural shaping rationale is partial.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no uncertainty anywhere — no indication of which concern methods it actually opened vs inferred, no hedge on whether all association options were confirmed; an agent inherits false confidence on details like the exact dependent: semantics on every association and the full method signature of HasUrl.

relationship.rationale: For each contract piece the answer states the binding relation explicitly: HasUrl supplies 'extract_url/extract_sha1/get_from_url/get_from_urls'; OptimizedImage is derived via 'has_many :optimized_images dependent: :destroy' and spawned through 'create_for'; OptimizedVideo holds 'belongs_to :upload AND belongs_to :optimized_upload class Upload' (dual FK explained); UploadReference is a 'polymorphic join' with 'ensure_exist!' — the connecting mechanism, not just the endpoint, is stated for each.

steps.step: Trace how an upload is created and processed

map_quality.rationale: The answer names the entrypoint (lib/upload_creator.rb:59), the storage chain (BaseStore#store_upload -> get_path_for_upload -> store_file at base_store.rb:12,124), and the fan-out (OptimizedImage inline during bake at cooked_processor_mixin.rb:461-481, ConvertVideo ASYNC at convert_video.rb:11, PullHotlinkedImages ASYNC at pull_hotlinked_images.rb:49,129, SyncAccessControlForUploads ASYNC) with INLINE/ASYNC flags throughout — an agent can follow this path with only small targeted lookups. A minor gap is that the direct-upload (ExternalUploadStub) pathway is only alluded to in the storage section without tracing its hand-off into UploadCreator.

specificity.rationale: Every layer is named with file:line: lib/upload_creator.rb:59, lib/validators/upload_validator.rb:5, lib/file_store/base_store.rb:12,124, s3_store.rb:32,86,48, optimized_image.rb:35,11, cooked_processor_mixin.rb:461-481,523, convert_video.rb:11, pull_hotlinked_images.rb:49,129, sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb:19 — no vague references to 'the upload code'.

justification.rationale: The answer labels each step INLINE or ASYNC but never states WHY heavy processing (video conversion, avatar thumbnails, hotlink pull, ACL sync) is deferred — no mention of keeping the HTTP request fast or avoiding blocking the user-facing path. Labeling is not explanation.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer is uniformly confident; it does not flag which store-dependent branches it did not open (e.g., the full direct-S3 multipart flow), whether the DistributedMutex on optimized-image creation was confirmed open, or that fan-out ordering relative to the save transaction was not verified. The agent would need to discover these gaps itself.

relationship.rationale: Within sections the answer shows explicit mini-chains — 'BaseStore#store_upload -> get_path_for_upload -> store_file' and 'after_create enqueue_video_conversion_job -> Jobs::ConvertVideo' — but the overall structure is four parallel lists (entrypoint, validation, storage_layer, fan_out) rather than one written end-to-end chain of the form file -> creator -> store -> variants/post-processing; the agent must infer the ordering across sections rather than reading a connected path.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

map_quality.rationale: All seven required groups are present with strong coverage: post cooking, storage/CDN, jobs, email, backup, theming, profiles, and plugin surface. However, the user merger (explicitly named in the rubric for user profiles and merge) is absent entirely, lib/site_setting_extension.rb for upload-typed site-setting hydration is not listed by name, and the site-icon manager is not called out distinctly.

specificity.rationale: The overwhelming majority of entries carry file:line and a per-entry phrase (e.g., 'lib/email/sender.rb:372-428 — attaches post.uploads / optimized_images inline, secure-image gating'), which is exactly the right level; the notable exception is lib/site_setting_extension.rb, which is never named, and a few entries are grouped into slash-separated pairs ('app/controllers/static_controller.rb / metadata_controller.rb') without individual lines.

justification.rationale: Grouping by area carries implicit WHY, and per-entry phrases mostly go beyond mere naming ('rewrites secure upload images in HTML emails', 'includes uploads in archive, stamps s3_base_url/s3_cdn_url metadata', 'orphan upload GC … destroy vs delete'); some entries in the storage and job groups describe WHAT rather than WHY the contract change would affect them.

uncertainty.rationale: The teammate highlights section explicitly names three non-obvious couplings (URL-to-path derivation, OptimizedVideo double FK, secure-status propagation) and calls out what 'a plain text search for Upload would miss', which is useful; but the answer never flags which areas were NOT fully opened or where the static pass might still leave gaps, so the agent cannot tell what still needs manual confirmation.

relationship.rationale: For the majority of obvious dependents the connecting relation is stated (concern names, belongs_to associations, storage-route via Discourse.store), including the non-obvious 'BaseStore#get_path_for_upload derives the storage path FROM upload.url'; but for the hardest non-obvious dependents the connection is weak: email/styles.rb's sha-map mechanism is named without explaining how it reaches Upload, lib/s3_inventory.rb's etag/path route to Upload is not stated, and lib/site_setting_extension.rb's upload-type hydration path is entirely absent.

steps.step: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer gives three well-populated lists (identity resolvers, currency guards, storage resolvers) with file:line throughout — an agent can find every named entry point without re-grepping. What is missing is a per-service trace: the rubric asks how post-processing, email, backup, and S3 reconciliation each confirm a real, current upload before acting, and the answer never walks through those call-sites in sequence; it inventories the primitives and leaves the agent to assemble the service-level picture.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every guard and resolver is named with an exact symbol and file:line (e.g., `Upload.consider_for_reuse — app/models/upload.rb:240`, `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload — lib/file_store/base_store.rb:118-154`, `check_secure_upload_permission — lib/secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17-20`); there is no vague 'the validation code' language.

justification.rationale: Inline parenthetical notes give partial WHY — '(rejects mismatched access_control_post / pre-secure uploads)', '(DistributedMutex on sha1, secure->signed_url else cdn_url)' — but the answer never consolidates the reason the whole guard layer exists (upload may be secure, missing, or on remote storage that requires a signed URL), leaving the agent to infer the design intent from scattered annotations.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer does not flag any path where a guard is absent or implicit — for instance, it does not note that the backup restorer or email sender may assume the upload record exists and skip a presence check, which is exactly the kind of gap the rubric asks to surface.

relationship.rationale: Guards and resolvers are categorized but never explicitly connected to the downstream effects they protect: `has_been_uploaded?` is listed but the answer does not say it gates `link_post_uploads` from linking foreign uploads; `perform_download`'s DistributedMutex + secure-branch is listed but not tied to which callers (email sender, cooked processor) depend on it before acting. An agent reading this still must manually trace which guard gates which dependent.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: All eight rubric areas are covered with explicit risk tiers (highest/medium/lower) and file:line throughout — BaseStore path coupling at base_store.rb:124-140, destroy cascade at upload.rb:53-64, secure URL/ACL at upload_security.rb and s3_store.rb:218-257, post cooking at cooked_processor_mixin.rb:207-591, S3 reconciliation at s3_inventory.rb:99-144, email at sender.rb:372-428, backup at uploads_restorer.rb:73-111, theming at theme_field.rb:173-180, plugin callbacks at upload.rb:96-118. The agent can pick up each area and start targeted lookups immediately.

specificity.rationale: Every risk tier entry names specific files, line ranges, and a concrete failure mode — e.g., 'clean_up_uploads.rb:52 destroy-vs-delete branch is sensitive' and 'OptimizedVideo holds two Upload FKs and tears down a second Upload on destroy (optimized_video.rb:5,47)'. No vague bucket labels like 'various lib helpers'.

justification.rationale: Each highest-risk entry explains the failure mode directly: path coupling 'can desync stored bytes from DB url across ALL stores', destroy cascade 'risks orphaned S3 objects or dangling FKs', and S3 reconciliation 'wrong path resolution can mass-mark uploads missing'. Medium and lower tiers give abbreviated but meaningful reasons ('depends on absolute_base_url/cdn_url/upload_path shape').

uncertainty.rationale: The answer is confident throughout with no explicit 'could not confirm' flags; 'lower_risk_but_verify' implies some hedging but never identifies which specific aspect is structurally uncertain or indirectly reached. An agent reading this might act on the lower-risk list without knowing which parts were verified vs inferred.

relationship.rationale: Highest-risk entries tie each dependent to its connecting path — BaseStore path 'derived FROM upload.url', secure status propagating 'from the original to optimized video uploads (upload.rb:511)', post cooking resolution through 'link_post_uploads / lightboxes / orphan-GC reference counting'. Medium-risk entries are briefer ('path_for/download!/sha1/secure?' for email) but still name the access method that couples each dependent to the contract.

map_quality.rationale: The map covers all seven areas (cooking, storage/CDN, jobs, email, backup, theming, profiles/avatars, plugin surface) with file:line throughout — e.g., upload.rb:53-64,212-217 for teardown, base_store.rb:124 for path coupling, clean_up_uploads.rb:30,51 for GC. Specs are enumerated by exact path with what each exercises; a teammate could act on this with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Files are named by full path with line ranges (app/models/upload.rb:17-34,53-64,219-289,492-523; lib/file_store/s3_store.rb; spec/lib/upload_creator_spec.rb etc.) rather than vague layer labels; the conditional spec additions ('if URL shape changes') also name exact spec files rather than 'add tests for email'.

justification.rationale: The grouped review lists in step7 are mostly bare file lists; WHY each file belongs to the edit — e.g., why theme_field.rb is in the theming group — is carried by earlier steps and the highlights section, not woven into the map itself. The two 'teammate-facing highlights' paragraphs do justify the highest-risk entries (get_path_for_upload URL coupling, teardown cascade, OptimizedVideo dual-FK) but the medium and lower sections lack per-file reasoning.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags conditional spec additions ('if URL shape changes') and notes storage/fixture refreshes are contingent on the URL contract, but does not explicitly call out which parts of the map it could not verify (e.g., whether all theme-setting upload-type references were found, or whether indirect plugin dependents are complete). The agent must infer confidence from prior steps rather than from explicit flags in step7.

relationship.rationale: The `review_or_touch_by_area` groupings do not state, per file, which relationship or path ties each file to the Upload contract change — only the has_post_upload_references.rb, clean_up_uploads.rb, and base_store.rb entries get explicit relationship statements (in highlights and step6), while theme_field.rb, email/sender.rb, or user_avatar.rb in step7 are listed without saying 'belongs_to :upload + SVG sprite download via store.path_for' or similar connecting chain.
